title: The Office Update
learningGoal: The learner will parse rapid conversational English to identify contracted Present Perfect auxiliaries ('I've', 'He's', 'They've') and isolate the subsequent V3 participles indicating task completion.
instructions: Listen to a boss checking on her workers in an office. They are speaking very quickly using 'Contractions' (I've instead of I have). Listen closely to what jobs they have already finished. Answer the 8 questions.

contentItems.text: Listen for the hidden 'Z' sound! If the boss says 'He's gone', she means 'He HAS gone'.

listeningGoal: Identify morphological contractions of 'have' and 'has' against subject pronouns in continuous speech.
audioScenario: A manager asking for status updates from her team.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Good afternoon, everyone. Let's check our progress.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: गुड आफ्टरनून, एवरीवन। लेट्स चेक आवर प्रोग्रेस।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: शुभ दोपहर, सब लोग। आइए हमारी प्रगति की जांच करें।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Amit, have you finished the report?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: अमित, हैव यू फिनिश्ड द रिपोर्ट?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: अमित, क्या तुमने रिपोर्ट पूरी कर ली है?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes, I've finished it. I've sent it to your email.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: येस, आईव फिनिश्ड इट। आईव सेंट इट टू योर ईमेल।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हाँ, मैंने इसे पूरा कर लिया है। मैंने इसे आपके ईमेल पर भेज दिया है।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Excellent. Where is John? I don't see him.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: एक्सीलेंट। वेयर इज़ जॉन? आई डोन्ट सी हिम।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: बहुत बढ़िया। जॉन कहाँ है? मैं उसे नहीं देख रही हूँ।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: He's left already. He's gone to the bank.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: हीज़ लेफ्ट ऑलरेडी। हीज़ गॉन टू द बैंक।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: वह पहले ही जा चुका है। वह बैंक गया है।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Okay. And what about the new computers?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: ओके। एंड वॉट अबाऊट द न्यू कंप्यूटर्स?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: ठीक है। और नए कंप्यूटरों के बारे में क्या?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: They've arrived! We've put them in the meeting room.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: देव अराइव्ड! वीव पुट देम इन द मीटिंग रूम।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: वे आ चुके हैं! हमने उन्हें मीटिंग रूम में रख दिया है।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Great work. It looks like we've completed everything.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: ग्रेट वर्क। इट लुक्स लाइक वीव कंप्लीटेड एवरीथिंग।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: बहुत अच्छा काम। ऐसा लगता है कि हमने सब कुछ पूरा कर लिया है।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes, we've had a very good day.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: येस, वीव हैड अ वेरी गुड डे।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हाँ, हमारा दिन बहुत अच्छा बीता (रहा) है।

audioTranscriptFull:
Boss: Good afternoon, everyone. Let's check our progress. Amit, have you finished the report?
Amit: Yes, I've finished it. I've sent it to your email.
Boss: Excellent. Where is John? I don't see him.
Amit: He's left already. He's gone to the bank.
Boss: Okay. And what about the new computers?
Amit: They've arrived! We've put them in the meeting room.
Boss: Great work. It looks like we've completed everything.
Amit: Yes, we've had a very good day.

questionSet.questionText: What does Amit say he has done with the report?
options: He's sent it to her email

questionSet.questionText: Listen closely: Does Amit say 'I have finished it' or 'I've finished it'?
When Amit says 'He's gone', what two words is he squishing together into 'He's'?
What has arrived at the office?
When Amit talks about the computers arriving, he says '_____ arrived!'.
Where have they put the computers?
When the boss says 'we've completed everything', what does 'we've' mean?

correctAnswers.q1: He's sent it to her email

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You perfectly decoded the native contractions! You heard 'He's' and instantly knew it meant 'He has' because of the V3 verb that followed it.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! This is very fast native speech. Play the audio on Slow mode. Listen to how 'I have' strings together into 'I've'. Native speakers do this to save time!
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good ear! Watch out for Question 4. Remember our rule: If 'He's' is followed by a V3 action like 'Gone', it MUST mean 'He has'! It cannot mean 'He is gone'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Boss: Good afternoon, everyone. Let's check our progress... (matches full transcript)

hintText: Pay attention to the tiny sounds. A 'v' sound at the end of a pronoun means 'have'. A 'z' sound means 'has'.
commonMistakes: Hearing 'He's left' and assuming it means 'He is left' (passive/adjective state) rather than Present Perfect 'He has left'.
